name: Chalabre, Camon and Castelreng
name_original: Chalabre, Camon et Castelrang en gravel – boucle
description: This difficult 45.5-mile gravel cycling loop near Chalabre offers 4001 feet of climbing and passes through the medieval town of Camon.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Chalabre offers routes through the Aude department's varied landscape, characterized by rolling hills, tranquil valleys, and scenic lakes. The region features a mix of open countryside, historic villages, and greenways, providing diverse terrain for cyclists. Routes often follow river courses or connect picturesque natural sites, with elevations ranging from gentle slopes to more challenging ascents. Best touring cycling routes around Chalabre The most popular touring cycling route is Lac de Montbel – Lake Montbel loop from Chalabre, a 15.7 miles (25.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 58 minutes to complete, circling the scenic Lac de Montbel. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Cathar Pyrenees Greenway: The Weavers' Path, a moderate 8.9 miles (14.4 km) path that follows a historic greenway with gentle gradients. Local touring cyclists also love the Chalabre, Camon and Castelreng, a 45.5 miles (73.2 km) trail leading through the countryside and historic villages, often completed in about 5 hours 24 minutes. Touring cycling around Chalabre is defined by rolling hills, tranquil lake shores, and historic greenways. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy family friendly bike trails to challenging cycling trails. The routes in Chalabre are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Chalabre's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Chalabre?
A: There are over 20 touring cycling routes in the Chalabre area, offering a variety of experiences from easy rides to more challenging ascents through the Aude department's varied landscape.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Chalabre?
A: The routes around Chalabre feature a diverse landscape, including rolling hills, tranquil valleys, and scenic lakes. You'll find a mix of open countryside, historic villages, and greenways, with elevations ranging from gentle slopes to more demanding climbs.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Chalabre offers routes suitable for all abilities. For an easy, family-friendly option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e929283873/cathar-pyrenees-greenway-the-weavers-path" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cathar Pyrenees Greenway: The Weavers' Path&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 8.9-mile (14.4 km) path following a historic greenway with gentle gradients.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes around Chalabre?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, there are several difficult routes. One such option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991155588/chalabre-camon-and-castelreng" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chalabre, Camon and Castelreng&lt;/a&gt;, a 45.5-mile (73.2 km) trail with significant elevation changes, typically completed in about 5 hours 24 minutes.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes around Chalabre?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as loops. A popular circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/29356362" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lac de Montbel – Lake Montbel loop from Chalabre&lt;/a&gt;, a 15.7-mile (25.3 km) trail that circles the scenic Lac de Montbel.

Q: What can I see along the touring cycling routes around Chalabre?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty and historic sites. Along your rides, you might encounter attractions such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/588630" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Labyrinthe Vert&lt;/a&gt;, the charming &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3174343" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medieval town of Camon&lt;/a&gt;, or the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3407492" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fontestorbes Spring&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about touring cycling in Chalabre?
A: The touring cycling routes in Chalabre are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 30 reviews. Cyclists often praise the tranquil lake shores, historic greenways, and the diverse options available for different ability levels.

Q: When is the best season for touring cycling in Chalabre?
A: The best seasons for touring cycling in Chalabre are typically spring and autumn. During these times, the weather is generally mild and pleasant, making for comfortable riding conditions through the region's open countryside and valleys.

Q: Is parking available for touring cyclists in Chalabre?
A: Yes, parking is generally available in and around Chalabre, particularly in the town center and near popular starting points for routes, such as those around Lac de Montbel. It's advisable to check local signage for specific parking regulations.

Q: Can I find cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the routes?
A: Many touring cycling routes in Chalabre pass through or near charming villages where you can find local cafes, bakeries, or small shops to refuel and enjoy a break. The region's historic villages often offer opportunities for refreshments.

Q: Are there less crowded touring cycling routes in Chalabre?
A: While popular routes like the Lac de Montbel loop can see more activity, many of Chalabre's routes traverse quieter country roads and greenways. Exploring some of the longer or more challenging routes, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20532837" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tougnets Pass – Puivert Lake. loop from Chalabre&lt;/a&gt;, can offer a more secluded experience.

Camon, in addition to having a remarkable natural heritage with more than five natural areas of ecological, faunal and floral interest, is especially known for its remarkable architectural heritage. The creation of the town dates back to 923. Ramparts, church and abbey as well as houses from the Middle Ages and the Renaissance follow one another for the lucky visitors who are lucky enough to pass by.
A nice quiet road, not too steep, with few cars and a beautiful view of the landscape!

faqs.question: How difficult is the Chalabre, Camon and Castelrang gravel loop?
faqs.answer: This is classified as a difficult touring cycling route, demanding a high level of fitness. It features a significant elevation gain of 1,220 meters over 73.2 kilometers, and includes unpaved segments that can be challenging, potentially requiring you to push your bike.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this gravel loop?
faqs.answer: The route lives up to its 'gravel' designation, incorporating unpaved sections. While the majority (66.0 km) is on roads, it also includes paths (2.80 km), streets (2.59 km), cycleways (1.69 km), and short sections of singletrack (120 m).

faqs.question: What points of interest will I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The loop passes through several notable places. You'll experience the charming old village of Chalabre, known for its relaxed atmosphere. A significant highlight is the Medieval town of Camon, celebrated for its architectural heritage including ramparts, a church, and an abbey. You'll also pass Between Castelreng and Bouriège, with the general area offering picturesque villages and tranquil valleys.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Chalabre, Camon and Castelrang gravel loop?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas aren't detailed, starting in Chalabre is a good option. It's described as a charming village with cafes and shops, suggesting it's a suitable place to begin and end your ride, likely with available parking.

faqs.question: Is this gravel loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as 'difficult' and requires a high level of fitness due to its length, significant elevation gain, and challenging unpaved sections. It is not recommended for beginners.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this gravel loop?
faqs.answer: The trail research does not specify the best time of year. However, given the 'difficult' classification and potential for unpaved sections, it's advisable to check local weather conditions, especially for rain which could make gravel sections more challenging. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant cycling temperatures.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The provided information does not specify whether dogs are allowed on this particular cycling route. It's best to check local regulations or signage regarding pets on trails in the Aude region before you go.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: There is no mention of any permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route. Generally, public cycling routes in France do not require permits, but it's always good practice to respect private property and local regulations.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or get refreshments along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the village of Chalabre is noted for having cafes and shops, making it a good spot for refreshments at the start or end of your loop. The Medieval town of Camon is also a significant settlement you pass through, which may offer options.

faqs.question: Does the route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several official trails, including the Véloroute du Piémont Pyrénéen 09 (Traversée de l'Aude), Des Vosges aux Pyrénées 13, vers le Col de Tougnets, Des Vosges aux Pyrénées, variante, Voie Verte en Pyrénées Cathares - Le Chemin des Filatiers, and Véloroute du Piémont Pyrénéen 08 (Mirepoix - Chalabre).

faqs.question: Are there any scenic viewpoints on the loop?
faqs.answer: While specific viewpoints aren't listed, the general area around Chalabre is known for picturesque villages and tranquil valleys. Some routes in the region offer views of the Pyrenees mountain range from higher elevations, so you might catch glimpses on this challenging loop.
